question:
Vern Jones climbed Mount Kilimanjaro at the age of 62. He was aware that he couldn't have succeeded without the help of the guides — young men who are often ill-equipped and poorly paid, working daily in dangerous conditions. His response was to found Kili Summit Club to raise money and provide resources for them.

What does Vern Jones' story teach us about helping others?
choices: That only rich people can help those in need
That one person's action can make a lasting difference to others
That mountain climbing is a dangerous sport to avoid
That charities are not effective in solving poverty

question: The sentence 'These young men, who are often ill-equipped themselves and poorly paid, work daily in dangerous conditions to help climbers achieve their goals.' contains:
choices: Only simple sentences
A dependent clause and an independent clause
Only independent clauses
A compound sentence with two main clauses

question: The sentence: 'This is then kept on the wall and can be used by strangers, who may not have enough money, to enjoy a slice of the delicious pies.' is an example of a/an ________ sentence.
choices: simple
compound
complex
fragment

question:
Tracy paid for the stranger's milk and other items and, after thanking her and asking her name, he was on his way.

This sentence is an example of:
choices: a simple sentence with one clause
a complex sentence with dependent and independent clauses
a compound sentence with coordinating conjunctions
a fragment with no main verb

question: Which of the following is a social issue?
choices: learning a new language
gender equality
reading books
watching documentaries

question: Which of the following social issues relates to people moving from one country to another?
choices: poverty
unemployment
immigration
racism

question: Which Jordanian Royal Family initiative helps promote sustainable development that does not harm the environment?
choices: Moving people out of poverty
Improving health facilities
Helping refugees find homes
Promoting sustainable development

question: What did Lisa take on to help build her business, which was a young person doing a job to get experience and to learn?
choices: a foundation
an intern
a commission
a proceeds

question: Lisa received ________ for her 24-hour fund-raising activity, which allowed her to open Sister Pie.
The word 'enforce' belongs to which word class?
choices: noun
adjective
verb
adverb

question: The word 'enforceable' belongs to which word class?
choices: noun
adjective
verb
adverb

question: The word 'enforcement' belongs to which word class?
choices: noun
adjective
verb
adverb

question: Equality ________ means that everyone has exactly the same rights.
choices: Equality
Equal
Equalize
Equally

question: Does anyone have any ________ ideas for the surprise party?
choices: bright
brightened
brighten
brightening

question: To find a solution I think we need to look at the problem from a different ________.
choices: angling
angle
angled
angles

question: Please ________ your thoughts on what you think went wrong.
choices: sharing
shared
share
shares

question:
'My grandmother taught me to cook.'
Rawan said that ________.
choices: her grandmother taught her to cook.
her grandmother had taught her to cook.
her grandmother teaches her to cook.
her grandmother has taught her to cook.

question:
'I have won two competitions last year.'
My brother said that ________.
choices: he had won two competitions the year before.
he has won two competitions last year.
he won two competitions last year.
he wins two competitions the year before.

question:
'We and a few other friends had a meeting last week.' (They say this)
They say ________.
choices: they and a few other friends had a meeting last week.
we and a few other friends had a meeting last week.
they and a few other friends have a meeting last week.
they and a few other friends has a meeting last week.

question:
'Stop what you are doing right now!'
She ordered us ________.
choices: stopped what we were doing right then.
stop what we were doing right then.
to stop what we were doing right then.
not to stop what we were doing right then.

question:
'It has been a successful day at work today.'
They said ________.
choices: it has been a successful day at work today.
it had been a successful day at work that day.
it was a successful day at work that day.
it would be a successful day at work today.

question:
'Could I ask you a few questions here and now?'
He asked ________.
choices: if he could ask me a few questions here and now.
if he could ask me a few questions there and then.
could he ask me a few questions there and then.
if could he ask me a few questions there and then.

question:
'They have been trying to find the answer to the maths problem.'
She said ________.
choices: they have been trying to find the answer to the maths problem.
they had been trying to find the answer to the maths problem.
they were trying to find the answer to the maths problem.
they tried to find the answer to the maths problem.

question:
Use the prompts to report: Osama said 'I'm not sure, but I might. Who else will be there?'
Osama replied that ________.
choices: he wasn't sure, but he will.
he isn't sure, but he might.
he wasn't sure, but he might.
he hasn't been sure, but he might.

question:
Rashed said: 'Salah and Talal will be going.' (Report this)
Rashed told him that ________.
choices: Salah and Talal would be went.
Salah and Talal would was going.
Salah and Talal will be going.
Salah and Talal would be going.

question:
'The report the newspaper published yesterday has caused a lot of discussion.'
Mr Bager said ________.
choices: the report the newspaper published yesterday has caused a lot of discussion.
the report the newspaper had published the day before had caused a lot of discussion.
the report the newspaper has published the day before had caused a lot of discussion.
the report the newspaper would publish the day after would cause a lot of discussion.

question:
'Everyone who has taken part in the beach clean-up today will come back next week.'
We told the journalists that ________.
choices: everyone who had taken part in the beach clean-up that day would come back the following week.
everyone who has taken part in the beach clean-up today will come back next week.
everyone who took part in the beach clean-up that day will come back next week.
everyone who had taken part in the beach clean-up today will come back the following week.

question:
'Abeer: It was that man who stole the money!'
Abeer accused ________.
choices: a man stealing the money.
the man about stealing the money.
a man of stealing the money.
a man for stealing the money.

question:
'Sorry I hurt you, Ahmad.' (Sami said this)
Sami apologised ________ hurting him.
choices: to
by
for
about

question:
'Ok, I cheated during the test.' (She admitted this)
She ________.
choices: agreed to cheating during the test.
admitted cheating during the test.
refused cheating during the test.
insisted on cheating during the test.

question: The verb 'warn' can be used with two patterns. Which two patterns does 'warn' follow?
choices: verb + that + clause AND verb + object + to-infinitive
verb + gerund AND verb + preposition + gerund
verb + to-infinitive AND verb + object + preposition + gerund
verb + that + clause AND verb + gerund

question: The verb 'promise' can follow which TWO patterns?
choices: verb + object + to-infinitive AND verb + preposition + gerund
verb + that + clause AND verb + to-infinitive
verb + gerund AND verb + object + that clause
verb + object + preposition + gerund AND verb + gerund

question: Which of the following phrases expresses a LESS STRONG opinion?
choices: Without a shadow of a doubt, ...
It goes without saying that ...
As far as I'm concerned, ...
Frankly, I think it's obvious that ...

question: Which question is used to challenge somebody else's opinion?
choices: 'I'm inclined to think that ...'
'Are you saying that ...?'
'As far as I'm concerned ...'
'I'm no expert, but ...'

question: What does the aspiration rule in pronunciation state about sounds /p/, /t/, /k/?
choices: They are always aspirated at the end of a word.
They are aspirated at the beginning of a stressed syllable before a vowel.
They are aspirated only when followed by another consonant.
They are never aspirated in English.

question: Which word contains an aspirated sound?
choices: clock
drop
repair
vitamins

question: What does the word 'foundation' mean?
choices: money paid to a salesperson for selling something
the total money received from selling or organising something
an organisation started for a specific purpose, such as research
a person who does a job to get experience

question:
Read this passage and answer the question:

In the past, the animals often attacked people living in the city, so the people found a way to deal with them: they cut holes in the city walls and threw food to the hyenas. They realised that the hyenas were no longer hungry because of this, and attacked people less often. Later, a farmer called Yusuf Salleh chose to do the same thing to protect his farm animals against the hyenas: he fed them each day, and it worked!

Why did Yusuf Salleh start feeding the hyenas?
choices: To attract tourists to his farm
To protect his farm animals against the hyenas
To earn money from the hyenas
To continue a family tradition started by his son

question:
Read this passage and answer the question:

Yusuf's son, Abbas Yusuf, has continued his father's tradition but in a slightly different way. The sight is so amazing that many tourists visit to watch him feed the hyenas (although it is too dangerous for them to participate in the feeding)!

Why can't tourists participate in feeding the hyenas?
choices: Because the hyenas don't like strangers
Because it is too expensive
Because it is too dangerous for them
Because Abbas Yusuf doesn't allow it

question:
Read this passage and answer the question:

I've been trying to persuade people in my community to help an environmental charity by donating just a few coins each week. At first people were not keen, but my persistence has paid off and now a lot of people give me a little money.

What does the phrase 'persistence has paid off' suggest about the author's experience?
choices: The author received money for being persistent.
Continuing despite difficulty led to eventual success.
The persistence made people angry at first.
The author gave up after the initial rejection.
